segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain smelled of hot stone and a kiln that had just shut its mouth. Caelin woke to Vex's palm over his mouth, her other hand clamped to his shoulder.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Don't make a sound,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she breathed.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Company."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in his right forearm tightened its pulse, not warning, not ease -- just braced. Coal-glow slid toward ember-red; the fine obsidian filaments beneath his skin crawled up toward the elbow like roots searching stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the lower passage -- the one they'd marked with chalk and left closed -- voices braided the air. Wrong, guttural, harmonized like things that had practiced speaking together inside the same throat.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Ember-born..."

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "...the chain calls..."

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "...submit or be consumed..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand left his mouth.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Up,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she hissed, and the camp came alive in a soft clatter of straps and breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana rolled to her feet, sword in one hand, the other kindling silver-gold. The light stuttered gold?silver?gold -- a tremor she smoothed with breath. Thornik's pack went from hum to urgent whine as needles spun.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's vine-marks dimmed to a determined green. Nyxara stood in a movement that wasn't one, purple aura guttering like a candle in wind. Durgan took the choke point, blades bare, shadow pooled neat at his boots -- too neat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Signatures?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik checked a gauge that vibrated his teeth.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Multiple. Same resonance as Thornwick -- stronger. Focused."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They'll smell the Ember,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, eyes on Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "A bonfire in a dark valley."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Can you dim it?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked, already moving to shield the center.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "No."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd tried. White sparks punished lies, and it punished denial just as fast.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we work with it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Caelin center-back. I hold the line. Thornik on my flank, steel first, toys second. Vex wide right. Elowen disrupt their approach. Nyxara -- "

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I'll... persuade,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara murmured, voice a soft knife.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan didn't wait for orders.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They know exactly where we are,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, jaw tight.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Kill fast, before the trap closes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The voices fell silent. The air drew a breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By first light that never reached this deep, the tunnels had learned their names.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum settled to a floor-note you only noticed when it stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust lifted off the stone in slow spirals, then hung as if waiting to be told where to fall.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand found the wall.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Metallic again,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she murmured.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Coin on the tongue."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The pull's stronger,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said. Three words. Nothing more.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's wardline thinned to chalk-pale, then steadied.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin felt the scale tighten -- warning without pain, the kind that comes just before it hurts.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The chamber ahead was the shape of a held breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Shadow clung to the facets and then decided to stand.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Five of them -- wrong-jointed, glass-slick, learning their limbs like newborns. They moved like puppets given too many strings. Joints bent a fraction wrong.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Skin was the color of cold parchment, stretched and shifting over bones that didn't respect anatomy. Eyes were black from lid to lid -- no iris, no white, just void that stared back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Their shadows moved first, crawling toward Caelin's arm with hungry, fingered curiosity.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When they spoke, three voices braided through each throat: the people they had been, something ancient and patient, and beneath both the grind of stone on stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Ember-born,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: they said together.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The Seal remembers. The Seal hungers."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Line,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, sword low.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Light, then edges. No heroes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik palmed two charges.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Edges I have. Heroes, sadly, I left at home."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex snapped, already moving.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Right,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara echoed, voice silk over wire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin took center because the path in his bones put him there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's edge bloomed black-violet -- ink spreading through obsidian water -- and snapped back to red-gold. It hurt different this time: not the compressive burn of claiming, not the constant ache he could now ignore.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: This was a hook, barbed and patient, tugging along the filament lattice as if it could pull the Ember out of him with steady, surgical malice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He staggered. Elowen caught his shoulder.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can sense it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice tight.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "A severing. I can't ease this."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we break the hand holding the hook,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, and ran.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He drew heat and starved it to a hard white seam across his palm, then cut the closest shadow at the ankle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It shrieked in three registers and folded -- but did not die.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain lit the scale a murderous red. He kept his hand closed anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana hit them like a wall of weather. Shield-first, a ward-wave plowing the nearest thrall backward so hard its shadow recoiled, hissing. Her sword came down in a clean kill-line --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her lips moved -- automatic prayer, the kind learned in bone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The answer struck from two mouths at once.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Protect the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Break the vessel to save the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her wardlight guttered, flared, guttered. Blood salted her tongue.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Choose,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she hissed -- to the voices, to herself. The blade came up anyway.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Fine. I choose now."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She stepped in and set her sword through the thing's shoulder -- and met resistance like cutting water that didn't agree to be cut. Light seared black flesh; it didn't fall. It grinned too wide -- teeth blooming like fungus where a mouth should stop.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Hard to kill!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she called.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "News flash,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, already gone from where she'd spoken.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Duck,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, and rolled a thimble charge.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It coughed instead of boomed; the echo slapped the chamber flat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow's edge tore like wet paper where the sound hit it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Acoustic shear,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he breathed.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Repeatable."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was a blur on the fringe: two quick stabs behind a knee and a tendon, twist and away. The thrall buckled correctly -- then its shadow lashed like a whip and caught her across the ribs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex hit stone, slid, swore without wasting air.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Shadows bite! Noted!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex went low, a fast insult of steel at what passed for a heel.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Trip the legs, quiet the mouth."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara didn't draw. She spoke a word that bent the lantern-angle; light arrived from the wrong direction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow flinched from illumination that shouldn't exist there.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Transposition,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, almost kindly.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stand in your proper place."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik roared -- a sound that came from under mountains -- and hit the hamstrung thrall with two axes like punctuation. Steel met neck. Head rolled. Its shadow screamed like glass breaking underwater and blew away.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "One!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he bellowed, beard alight with reflected ember.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara didn't shout. She breathed, and indecision bloomed in two corrupted minds. One raked at the wrong air where Caelin had stood; the other hesitated in a three-second loop of doubt.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, soft but absolute.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana obliged. Silver-gold through the throat; the shadow boiled like ice in summer and was gone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The fourth rose for Vex's throat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan was there. No shout, no flourish -- steel where it had to be. His forearm took the rake that would have opened her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He shoved it back two steps and did not pursue.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The last turned from the fighters and stretched too many fingers toward Caelin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ember-born,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: three voices purred.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The chain is mercy. Let us hold your fire. We will take the pain away."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale chose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Heat flowered in his chest like a furnace door thrown wide. It wasn't temperature; it was pressure finding a path. His right arm lifted with a puppet's surety. Obsidian hex flared white. The lattice lit like molten wire from palm to collarbone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't aim. Didn't choose. The Ember chose him as an answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Red-gold plasma ripped the air. Granite vitrified to glossy black where it touched. The smell hit hard -- hot glass, burnt ozone, iron filings on the tongue.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The thrall didn't burn. It unmade. Flesh evaporated. Bone sifted to ash. The shadow screamed in three notes and was not.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three seconds. Then the door slammed shut.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain took the room.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The remaining shadows collapsed into behaving dark, dragging cold with them that wasn't temperature so much as absence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They said nothing more. They only learned the shape of the group and thinned into the walls.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sound returned in layers -- first to the floor, then to the air, then, last of all, to breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin flexed scorched fingers. The scale tallied pain and settled to coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sound wavered, distant and too loud. His heart rattled a panicked rhythm. The plate in his arm dropped from white-hot to furious red, the filaments pulsing like infected seams. His lungs forgot how. He went to a knee on a floor that looked like a mirror still cooling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen was there, palms hovering. Green light reached for him -- and guttered, the smell of scorched leaves sharp and wrong. A thin edge of one vine-mark blackened on her skin as she pulled back.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can't,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered, horrified.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Post-cast residue burns my connection. I can ease your baseline, not this."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "S'okay,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he managed through his teeth.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You told me."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex slid in hard, fingers at his pulse, pupils, breath.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not dying,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said briskly.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Shouldn't cast again for hours -- maybe a day -- unless we want him to."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She made a leveling motion with her hand.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her joke was a little too thin to be a joke. He almost laughed anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana kept her hand on the ward until it stopped shaking. When she finally sheathed the blade, her mouth was set against words.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex wiped her knife clean and didn't thank Durgan. She didn't not thank him, either.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara smoothed her coat as if dirt were an opinion, then watched Serana a fraction too long.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik counted fuses he hadn't used and grinned like a man who'd just learned his math could kill.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He glanced at Caelin's arm -- coal-glow again, the ember-motes drifting slow.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Back to baseline,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, as if noting a reading.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Coal means steady. Ember-red means spent. Black-violet means -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped himself.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Well. We know what black-violet means."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the mouth of the passage, Durgan hadn't moved an inch. He kept the retreat open, blades ready -- eyes trained not on the dark beyond, but on the lattice burning under Caelin's skin. His shadow flexed like a dog that had caught a scent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flattened it with will and left crescent bruises through his trousers where his fingers anchored him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum returned, deeper, almost pleased.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Not a voice,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, listening to the stone.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Not yet."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They took stock fast because there was no slow left.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex: moving, rib-shot, functioning at perhaps 70%.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik: winded, uncut, checking his instruments with shaking hands.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen: whole in body, less in magic -- one vine-mark dim at the edge, blackened where she'd tried to touch the post-cast burn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara: unmarked, a faint frost halo gone when she blinked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan: coiled too tight, shadow obedient by force, four crescent marks pressed into his thigh through his trousers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana: connection steadied, a tiny flicker when she turned to speak, lips still tasting copper from the split counsel.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin: vertical by stubbornness, the scale back to coal-glow, pain settling to the constant ache Elowen could ease later -- if it wasn't fresh-burn.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They'll follow the Ember,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, breaking the silence.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Distance won't matter."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Surface,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex started -- then glanced toward the south, toward Millbrook and the scattered hamlets tucked under these ridges. Her jaw tightened.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "No. Not with a beacon on two legs. I hate the idea, but... containment."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The Undervault,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik offered, already pulling the battered journal from his coat. Pages crackled, stained with old blood and older ink.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Pre-Concord passages. Old dwarf-wards. My grandfather mapped entries and marked the Deep Hall. Wards there might confuse whatever is sniffing us."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Safer for civilians,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, jaw set.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not for us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's gaze went inward, the way it did when she listened to the Weave. The green of her tattoos pulsed faintly, then dimmed.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The deep places are hurt. Wounded things can be generous. Or angry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's fingers twitched toward a blade, then away -- a motion so fast only Vex caught it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Safer to be hunted in stone than among farms."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They looked to Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale warmed -- not push, not pull. Waiting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He thought of Thornwick. Of glass bones where houses had stood. Of Mira's small hand clutching his ruined cloak.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Undervault,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We don't bring this down to doorsteps."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Excellent,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, already shouldering weight, adjusting straps with practiced efficiency.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I've always wanted to risk my life on decayed ventilation and ancestral pride."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Lead on,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, checking her daggers with methodical precision.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "If a ward eats us, I'm haunting you."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Queue forms to the left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, finding his cheer again despite everything.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ancestral disapproval is first-come, first-served."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik found the seam in the wall by feel -- three high, two low, one center -- and an ancient hinge sighed cold air into their faces. The breath of the Undervault tasted of iron, dust, and old smoke that had forgotten its fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A tight spiral took them down past shelves of fossilized lichen that glowed just enough to make shadows mean. The scale's coal-glow mixed with green-blue, turning everything a strange bruised purple.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Their boots found perfect dwarf-cut treads, each one precisely placed, worn smooth by centuries of use. Breath plumed in the cold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They came to a three-way junction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik thumbed brittle pages, following his grandfather's diagrams with one finger while holding his lantern close.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Left is sealed. Collapse, bad air, and a page of do not in runes only grandmothers use."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He squinted at faded ink.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Right's toward the forges -- likely flooded. Straight is the Deep Hall. Strongest wards. Multiple exits."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "'Should be' is the most dangerous phrase,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex muttered, testing the air currents with a wetted finger.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Aye,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said cheerfully.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Shall we?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They walked until silence felt heavier than noise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain had gone from predator to cathedral -- huge, patient, and indifferent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Every sound came back doubled, as if the tunnels were practicing conversation.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By the time they moved again, their footsteps felt borrowed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bridge waited with the patience of stone made to sing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Below, the dark had depth -- thick enough to hold a choir.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Single file,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, testing the first plank with her boot.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Slow. No idle noise."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik swallowed a theory about resonant architecture. Vex swallowed a curse about trusting ancient engineering.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said nothing and stepped where he was told.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The first footfall rang a low bell -- pure, round, unhurried.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The second answered a third below, like harmony remembering itself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By Caelin's fourth step the bells had learned his weight and offered a scale that climbed in perfect intervals.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The plate in his arm kept time despite him, pulsing in rhythm with the bells -- coal-glow brightening fractionally with each step.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, Nyxara's heel grazed a loose shard.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The note that answered came bent, then split into two, then four -- ugly, eager, dissonant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The span trembled. Cracks webbed outward from where her foot had landed, then held -- barely.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't improvise,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex hissed without turning, knuckles white on the rope.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Tragic,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, adjusting her footing with careful precision.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I excel at it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the chasm rose an answering chord -- soft at first, then full enough to live in. Harmonics layered upon harmonics, building a cathedral of sound in the empty air.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's eyes went wide.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Roots sing under storms,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "This isn't that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's hand hovered over his tuning fork, then curled to a fist.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "This is engineered. Intentional. The entire bridge is an instrument."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin tasted metal and old ash. The scale warmed without pain, as if listening back, recognizing something in the music.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached a split in the span: two narrow runs diverging for ten feet before rejoining, creating a gap where no single person could span both paths.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Pairs, or it drops us,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, staring at the bell spacing carved into each path.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "It wants balance, not mass. Weight distribution."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Vex with Nyxara,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana ordered, already assessing.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Thornik with me. Caelin in the center. Durgan anchors."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex clicked her tongue.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You do love a trust exercise."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara smiled.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I adore being trusted."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan took the rear without comment, his shadow pooling perfectly still despite the chasm wind.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The twin paths learned their pairs fast -- two notes for each step, tuned just off a perfect fifth. When Serana's breath hitched mid-crossing, her note wavered. The bridge shuddered.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Hold,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said -- more plea than order.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She held. The bells forgave her, the dissonance resolving back to harmony.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A hairline seam opened under Vex's foot without warning.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't scream; she vanished down to the knee, fingers scrabbling for purchase. Her knife hand caught stone, blade sinking into a crack with desperate precision.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara didn't reach for her. She changed the angle of her shadow with a subtle gesture, fingers tracing sigils in the air.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For one breath, light came from the wrong direction; the stone remembered being whole. The seam closed just enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex kicked free, hauled up with trembling arms, swore once -- the rare, sincere kind that meant actual fear.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them Durgan's note didn't change. He hadn't moved. Hadn't even shifted his weight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The last bell rang itself when Caelin stepped off the stone, a final perfect tone that lingered in the air long after it should have faded.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the corridor opened into a dome vast enough to hold weather.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nine pillars ringed the space, each cut with a different pattern of facets that caught light and swallowed it. They didn't glow. They absorbed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The quiet here was thicker than dark.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Resonant sink,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik breathed, voice dropping to a whisper instinctively.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It drinks sound."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then whisper,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Or think louder than you speak."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's wardlight dimmed to a candle's stubborn core.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "This place keeps oaths. Or breaks them."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara walked the nearest pillar, fingertip hovering, never touching.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Nine doors that forgot they were doors."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The pull's stronger,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said. Three words. Nothing more.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The plate brightened from coal to ember.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not pain -- attendance.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin laid two fingers against the nearest pillar. It did not warm. It did not sing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It remembered a heat so old the stone no longer called it heat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In that heartbeat he saw a hand not his own pressing this same facet; a voice not his own saying a name he didn't know how to repeat. The vision smelled of forge-fire and ozone, tasted of copper and purpose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He pulled back. Taste of copper. Taste of thunder about to happen.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I think it knows the order,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "I don't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A note rose from somewhere beneath the dome -- lower than any bell on the span, the kind that rearranges ribs and makes bones remember they're temporary.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The nine pillars answered by not answering. Each drank the sound and gave back silence, creating a void where the note should have echoed.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Echoes below,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, instruments beginning to hum in sympathy.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Not echoes,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen murmured, one hand pressed to her sternum as if feeling the vibration there.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Something that expects us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stood in the quiet a moment longer, counting breaths they hadn't meant to hold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the pull in Caelin's arm tightened, and the path chose left.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Deep Hall opened not gradually, but all at once -- as if a cathedral of stone had been hiding an arm's length beyond the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pillars like old oaks rose from an obsidian floor polished to mirror.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Bas-reliefs coiled up each: dwarves and dragons working side by side; forges burning in colors metal shouldn't make; the nine pedestals carved and blessed, each one distinct in its geometry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the crown of every pillar, chains looped around a shape no artist would define -- something that might have been a lock, or a heart, or both.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Along the walls, nine alcoves expressed their schools in stone:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Evocation: facets that multiplied light until it looked like heat, angular and aggressive.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Abjuration: nested bevels, each guarding the next in concentric protection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Conjuration: empty arches promising somewhere else, frames without destinations.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Divination: spirals your eyes couldn't follow without falling, probability made geometry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Enchantment: curves that made attention lean, seductive and subtle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Illusion: surfaces that adjusted when you didn't look, never quite the same twice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Necromancy: a hollow; an absence; a worn edge where something used to be.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Transmutation: rough stone flowing to glass mid-surface, frozen mid-change.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Destruction: edges broken on purpose to prove a point, jagged and defiant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In the center, a round granite table big enough for a parliament. Dust softened the shine. No footprints but theirs.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The wards held,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik breathed, watching his scanner's needles settle to a calm green.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Active peace. Saints and stone -- this place is safe."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana still paced the perimeter -- old habits dying hard. Her light swept high vaults and alcoves; nothing moved but breath and dust motes dancing in the ward-glow. Vex tested for pressure plates out of reflex, found none. Elowen's voice came out as reverent as prayer.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stone remembers bargains."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan paused at the threshold. The carved runes flickered -- curious, then tolerant.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Uncomfortable,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said honestly, and crossed. His shadow tried to widen past his boots; the ward-light nudged it back like a shepherd dog redirecting wayward sheep. He set his jaw. It obeyed.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We rest,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana decided.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Pairs on watch. No solos."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They burned a small, clean fire in a shallow stone cradle with invisible vents -- Thornik tapped the pillar and grinned:

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We make smoke go where we want. Grandfather's diagrams called this 'breathstone.' Channels run through the walls."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air tasted faintly of oil and old ash; fresher scents -- leather, metal polish, juniper from a scrap of kindling -- cut through the mineral cold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ate dried meat and hard cheese. They checked steel, adjusted straps, cleaned blades. They didn't talk much at first.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin sat with his back to a pillar beneath Evocation, watching the facets multiply the firelight into something that looked almost like the flames he carried.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale had cooled to its steady coal-glow; the constant ache was back -- the kind Elowen could ease when it wasn't fresh-burn. He flexed his hand. The lattice answered, not offended, not appeased.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: You'll know when you can pay again, it seemed to say.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hoped his body would warn him before his bone-marrow did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First Watch: Caelin & Vex

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They kept it simple: steel on knees, backs to stone, ears tuned to the empty halls. The scale pulsed with his heart, ember drifting like slow fish under dark glass.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That thing is you now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, not unkind, studying the plate where it lay flush in his skin.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Optional to like it. Not optional to learn it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He huffed a laugh.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It calls me out when I lie. When anyone does."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He nodded at his arm.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "White spark. Like thorns under the skin."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Handy and infuriating."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She studied him with those mismatched eyes.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "For what it's worth -- you didn't hesitate when it acted. Courage or stupidity."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Which do you vote?"

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Yes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She eased a breath around her ribs, wincing slightly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Brave people do stupid things with better reasons."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "And you joke so you don't scream."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't get romantic. Joking also annoys monsters."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed quiet. Truth accepted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They sat in companionable silence after that, listening to the fire pop and the mountain breathe.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Second Watch: Serana & Thornik

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's hands rested in prayer form, fingers laced. No words came. Her lips moved occasionally but made no sound. Thornik pretended to tune his scanner while watching her not-pray.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Still split?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he asked gently after a long silence.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Protect yesterday. Sacrifice today."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mouth crooked, humorless.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I can follow orders. I cannot follow contradictions."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "In a good system, contradictions signal a changed input."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tapped his grandfather's journal.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "He wrote: Balance isn't the absence of cost. It's paying without bankrupting the world."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Doesn't make it easier."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said softly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "But it makes it necessary."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her light flickered gold?silver?gold again; she steadied it with a breath and nodded, accepting the truth if not the comfort of it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They sat watching the shadows dance on ancient carvings, and neither spoke of the weight they carried.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Third Watch: Nyxara & Elowen

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Silence sat between them like a cat that owned the room.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Your magic is... angled,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said at last, breaking the quiet.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "So is yours,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara replied, head tilted with feline curiosity.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Yours bends toward growth and cycles. Mine bends toward... consent, of a kind."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Do you trust it?"

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Trust implies parity."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A small, tired smile.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I honor the bargains I make."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "I'm losing ground,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said softly, touching the edge of the blackened vine-mark with careful fingers.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "When he casts, I can't help. I burn."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Then plan for loss,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said. Not cruel. Precise.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Decide who you are when the Weave doesn't answer."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen looked at the Necromancy alcove without fear, studying the hollow where something had been carved away.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I don't know."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Decide anyway."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A frost halo settled on Nyxara's lashes for a heartbeat and melted, leaving tiny droplets like tears that weren't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen noticed. Said nothing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Fourth Watch: Durgan (breaking the pair rule)

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He took the arch alone and didn't wake the person assigned to him. Not defiance -- necessity. When he slept, his shadow got ideas.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He kept it small, neat, obedient by force, the way you keep your breathing even under a predator's gaze. The scale's distant glow tugged at his periphery like a road that always slopes downward, whispering promises in a language he refused to learn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Walk away, a voice inside offered. Lose yourself in the dark. Stop being a danger.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't. Instead he squeezed four half-moons into his thigh through wool and felt the pain anchor him. Added four more next to the earlier set, a constellation of control etched in bruises.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His shadow moved anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not far -- an inch, maybe two, stretching toward the coal-glow at the edge of Caelin's bedroll. He caught it the moment he felt the resistance ease, snapped it back with a breath so sharp it hurt his chest, and held it there. It obeyed. Barely.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He made reaching boring. Made it cost. But it had moved on its own, and they both knew it.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "How long,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he thought, not asking anyone,

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "before I can't hold it still?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ward-lines along the threshold pulsed once -- assessing, not judging. They recognized restraint when they saw it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't move.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin woke to a sound his bones recognized before his mind did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not weather. Not rockfall. The counting of something chained far below.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale answered with a brighter coal-glow; ember drift quickened inside the obsidian. Around the circle, heads lifted one by one. The sound came again, nearer -- or just more focused, as if attention had turned their direction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud-thud-thud-thud --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's needles jittered from calm green to anxious yellow.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Movement. Multiple. Below us. Above us. Possibly... through us?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Any other good news?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked, already on her feet, daggers appearing in her hands.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The ventilation is superb,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, shouldering his pack with practiced speed.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And I'm very attached to my head."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the three corridors -- the sealed, the flooded, the safe -- stone began to move. Grinding. Opening. The air drew a breath that wasn't theirs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana rose fully armored, sword in hand, voice calm to keep the pulse down in others.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We move now. Thornik on point. Same formation. If a ward asks for courtesy, we give it. If something tries to herd us -- "

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We bite the hand,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, rolling a shoulder and wincing as her ribs protested.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Then run."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin stood. The ache was a live thing in his bones. Elowen's palm hovered near his arm -- she tested the air for fresh-burn and found none. Relief drifted in like cool rain; the pain stepped back a pace, manageable again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He swallowed gratitude, because speaking it would make it real, and he didn't know how many more times he'd get it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan slid to rear-guard. His shadow pooled obedi --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No. It pressed at the edges like tide against a seawall. He smoothed it with breath and didn't look at Caelin, didn't acknowledge the way it wanted to reach toward the coal-glow in the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara passed the Evocation alcove and paused for a heartbeat, head tilted. The scale gave a silver shimmer -- a brief halo that said another magic is looking back. She smiled to herself and moved on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stepped toward the straight corridor. Behind them, in the polished seal of the Deep Hall's floor, their reflections delayed by a single blink -- there, then gone, as if the stone was deciding whether to remember them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The heartbeat below picked up, counting them like beads on a string.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They went up.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved quickly after that, not running -- no one wanted to find out what would give chase -- but with the shared precision of people who'd heard a door close behind them and didn't know whose house they were in.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The tunnels felt steeper on the way out, as if the mountain had grown new muscles while they weren't watching. Gravity pulled harder, or perhaps exhaustion made it feel that way.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Walls that had been smooth now carried shallow grooves -- fresh, unfinished work, stone still warm under fingertips that brushed too close.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik muttered calculations under his breath, consulting his instruments.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Stress fractures don't self-heal. Something's rewriting geology."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex:

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Tell it to stop."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Would if I knew the syntax."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Try screaming louder."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She smiled. No one else did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the first junction they hit a dead end that hadn't existed before. The floor sloped upward in a spiral of newly-cooled glass, the surface too perfect to be natural, too warm to be old.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana touched the seam where new met old.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's sealing itself behind us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara looked at the closing curve, studying it with professional interest.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Or ahead of us. Perspective is the first casualty of divine architecture."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin set his palm against the wall; the scale pulsed once -- recognition, not alarm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's guiding, not trapping."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Comforting,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, checking over her shoulder at the way they'd come.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Until it stops."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The second tunnel forked into shadow. A draft hissed through like breath through teeth, carrying the smell of deep earth and older dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana drew light. It came thin and trembling, barely enough to push back the shadows.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She tried again. The radiance stuttered, guttered, and went out like a candle in wind.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I... can't feel the source."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her voice was calm because she'd trained it

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: to be, but her hand shook slightly before she lowered it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's reply was all sugar.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You're running on faith, darling. Check your reserves."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's tone cut sharper than intended. The warmth of the scale spilled into his voice, unasked.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "She's tired, not empty."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana looked at him -- really looked -- and he saw in her eyes the flicker of gratitude and shame tangled together like roots and chains.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I'll manage."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed a narrow chute where the roof had already dropped its first warning stones -- small ones, pebbles that clattered down like dice announcing the mountain's intentions.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik went first, hammering pitons into half-molten rock with steady, practiced strikes. Each blow echoed differently, telling him what the stone would hold and what it wouldn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway up, the air shifted -- pressure collapse, no sound, just a sudden absence -- then the wall shuddered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin felt the pull in his arm flare white-hot.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Move!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ran on instinct. The rear half of the tunnel folded inward, stone sighing like something exhaling dust and disappointment. Rock met rock with the sound of finality, sealing the way they'd come.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When the noise settled, the way back was gone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik stared at the new wall, panting.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Correction: rewriting geology in real time."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex pressed her hand against the fresh barrier. Still warm.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "So we go forward or we stay here until we're decoration."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Forward,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, voice steady though her light had guttered out entirely.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Always forward."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The climb stole what little rhythm remained in their breathing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's right arm ached with the memory of the sigils -- hot where the rest of him was cold. The filaments crawled beneath his skin like they were searching for something they'd lost.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen fell into step beside him without being asked.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "You're shaking."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It's the pull."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then push back."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He managed a half-laugh.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "That easy?"

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "But start somewhere."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She brushed her hand near his sleeve; the ache dulled. Not gone -- just bearable. The green of her tattoos flickered, dimmed slightly, another small price paid.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Thank you."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Later,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "When we're out."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them Nyxara glanced at Durgan's shadow -- still too obedient, too smooth, like a dog trained to sit perfectly still while its master walked away.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You hear it too?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she asked quietly.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The hum?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He shook his head once.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Footsteps."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Whose?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't answer. Didn't look at her. Just kept walking with that deliberate, measured pace that suggested if he stopped, he might not start again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When she looked again, his shadow was holding perfectly still while his boots moved -- a fraction too much control, the kind that comes from fighting something every single second.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At last the tunnel widened enough for rest.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik struck a flame that barely caught, nursing it like a dying thing. The light painted their faces in ochre and exhaustion, hollowing cheeks and deepening shadows.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex divided rations without asking; she had counted before anyone noticed they were running low. Dried meat, harder cheese, a swallow of water each. She moved with the efficiency of someone who'd learned hunger as a child and never forgotten the lesson.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana knelt apart, tracing the sigil of her order in ash on the ground. The mark wouldn't hold shape. The dust slid away from her fingers as if the mountain itself rejected the prayer. She tried again. Again the ash refused her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin watched but didn't interrupt.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Some prayers, he thought, were best left unanswered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A faint tremor moved through the stone like a muscle tensing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Their gear jingled once, softly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the hum changed key -- dropping a full tone, deeper, more present.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's head snapped up.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "That's not us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen closed her eyes, listening with her whole body the way trees listen to approaching storms.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Something old is turning over in its sleep."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex sheathed her knife slow, deliberate.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Let's not be the alarm clock."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed the last stretch in silence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Snowlight filtered down from a crack above -- thin, colorless, precious as air. The temperature dropped with every step, breath turning to visible ghosts in the suddenly frigid air.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When they emerged onto open rock, the air hit like resurrection: cold, clean, cruelly bright after so long in the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin turned back once. The tunnel mouth had already begun to glass over, edges smoothing and sealing, faintly glowing from within with a light that pulsed in time with the distant counting below.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He couldn't tell if it was sealing itself shut or lighting a path back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Either way, it watched them go.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "The mountain remembers,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen whispered.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Question is -- what?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Wind met them like a blade honed on snow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The world was all angles -- black rock, white glare, a sky so close it felt like a lid pressing down on the peaks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Breath came thin and loud in their own ears, each inhalation a small victory against the altitude.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge ran like a spine, dropping to glazed couloirs on either side -- sheets of ice that fell away into clouds and nothing. One wrong step and gravity would have opinions.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Far ahead, the flame-path in Caelin's vision brightened to a fine wire, then split along the crest -- two threads, left and lower, right and steep.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He blinked; the right-hand thread tugged harder. Not safer. Just certain. The kind of certainty that didn't care about safety.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Two ways,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then there's only one,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex answered, already squinting into the glare, reading the terrain with the practiced eye of someone who'd survived by always choosing the less-bad option.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sunlight cut through high cloud and made three mock-suns at the horizon -- halos stacked like coins, a parhelion that shouldn't have been so clear at this time of day.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a moment the middle ring threw nine spokes across the glare, a trick of ice crystals and angle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They watched until the spokes thinned and died, melting back into ordinary light.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Pretty,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, too softly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I hate it."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Atmospheric phenomenon,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Ice crystals refracting -- "

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I know what it is,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik interrupted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Doesn't make it less ominous."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Light for the edge,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, lifting a hand as they prepared to traverse the exposed ridge.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nothing came.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She breathed, steadied, tried again. The wardline stuttered and guttered like breath caught in a throat, flickering silver-gold-silver before dying completely.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Source is... thin,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice even by force of will.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Can't hold it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's smile didn't travel to her eyes.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Altitude does that to certainty."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Enough,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said -- quieter than anger, firmer than plea.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We move slow. No miracles, just rope."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana nodded once and lowered her hand. That, too, was a kind of prayer -- accepting limitation, working within it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They roped in -- Thornik's knots neat as script, each one tested twice. Vex testing every anchor with insults and physics, trusting neither completely.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge narrowed to two boot-widths and a faith wider than comfort.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan took rear without comment. He walked like a man who had been here in other lifetimes and found the drop repetitive. His shadow stayed small, obedient, costly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, a gust hit sideways -- a wall of wind with malice in it. Elowen's heel skated on rime; Caelin felt the rope jar tight across his chest, fibers groaning.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Lean in,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, not sure who needed it. Maybe himself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They did. The gust passed. The ridge became a ridge again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara altered the angle of her shadow with a single, almost lazy wrist-turn -- subtle magic, barely noticeable.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For one breath the glare softened where their eyes needed it most, giving them a moment of relief from the sun-blindness.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex saw it and didn't comment. She only moved faster, wanting to be off the exposed spine before the mountain changed its mind.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Shelter,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said, three heartbeats later.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not warning. A direction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They followed his chin-tilt to a bite taken out of the lee side -- a snow-scooped alcove backed by stone, protected from the worst of the wind.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crouched out of the wind, bodies grateful for even this small mercy. Fire was a joke up here where oxygen barely existed; Thornik didn't try. They huddled together for warmth instead.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana sat with her back to rock and closed her eyes like someone setting down a weight too heavy to carry any longer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin heard the whisper before he saw her jaw tighten -- the overlapping threads of two instructions that would never agree, playing in an endless loop.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Protect the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Offer the bearer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her fingers found the chalk tucked in her belt and snapped it cleanly in two. The pieces fell into the snow and were immediately buried by a fresh gust.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I can walk,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, eyes still shut.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "I can't promise more."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's enough,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't add, For now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex distributed a stingy fistful of dried fruit as if paying out wages.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Don't die; I'll have to redo headcount."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik chewed slowly, trying not to look at the halos still staining the horizon like cosmic warning signs.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Quantitatively speaking, we're under-supplied on miracles."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara watched the way the wind curled around Caelin's forearm, as if the air had opinions about ownership.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Quantitatively speaking,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she murmured,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "we're over-supplied on audience."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan didn't sit. His shadow behaved itself. That felt like a decision that cost him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge underfoot hummed -- two notes a tone apart, beating slow against each other in a rhythm that made teeth ache.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik touched stone with a bare palm.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "That's not weather."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen listened with her whole body, the way she'd learned to read the forest's moods.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Something turned over below us."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. The pull in his arm was no longer a suggestion. It was a command.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one argued.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved into a light that felt older than noon, shadows stretching wrong directions, the sun pale and distant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mock-suns had faded, but the taste of metal hadn't -- copper and ozone, the flavor of lightning about to strike.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the wind erased their prints in a single pass, as if the mountain wanted no record they'd been there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the path narrowed and began to sing -- a low, thrumming note that rose from stone or sky or the space between.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana opened her hand for light one more time and found none. She closed it before anyone saw, fingers curling into a fist.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But Caelin saw. And said nothing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Before they moved, Vex ran her count aloud -- four charges spent, one blade chipped, three wounds that needed proper dressing when there was time. Thornik tapped the scale of remaining fuses against his thigh: six left.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said nothing, but turned one wrist up; two more vine-marks had darkened at the edges, thin black lines where the green should have been. Serana's wardlight was gone and hadn't come back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked at his arm. Coal-glow. Steady. Not spent, not surging -- just present.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Still here,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, to no one in particular.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They descended.
